item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ooh, very good spot- will edit now. Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Same here. Upvoted, but I read the whole thing and still don't have a clue what the central mind control thingy does.

Consistency issue:
The artifact list says 4 are known and contained, but only 3 are given.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm upvoting because I love the attention to detail given in lost civilization scips (which are always my all time favorites), but I have to say, I feel like I completely missed the significance of the blueprint, and the final O5 collapsible did an exceedingly poor job of explaining why it had to be hidden so only O5s could access it (to me, at least. I may have completely missed something somewhere)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What an amazing adventure and solid read. In terms of execution, I love how you knew instinctively that long complex sentences would draw people away, leaving very simple yet concise sentences to lead the long-form narrative.

Extremely effective at keeping the reader's attention throughout and has taught me a lesson on what to and not to do in draft construction.

+1
mortimer

for reminding me of that one quote: "This just sounds like slavery with extra steps."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Tufto scips really immerse you: the carefully constructed prose, tight tone, and thoughtful use of the anomaly results in the overall aesthetic. Enjoyable as always. +1

That said, I do agree with ARD that the ending was not as satisfying as I would have liked. I feel like more analysis into the use and consequences of the Spire technology/project could've made the message stronger. Definitely a good use of the Thaumiel class though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The ending isn't particularly satisfying (though lord knows I've done the same thing), but the core motif is striking enough to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh Town of Johannes
Your spell is upon us,
Your spell and your lust
The dust chokes above and it chokes down below
But they from near and far come
I don’t what they’re looking for
I don’t what they’re looking for

Frankly, this entire thing is a mess and I'm really not happy about it. But I'm fed up with the damn thing and I may as well post given the effort I put into it.

With thanks to the following:

Uncle Nicolini
Rounderhouse
KindlyTurtleClem
Sepiafishie
The Great Hippo

The image is licensed CC-BY-SA 4.0, and belongs to Wikipedia author Dasturias. It can be found here.
